$comment: Known-OK event_id pairs whose TSV content overlaps >=80% but are legitimately separate entries (e.g. same venue listed under two series). Used by scripts/check-duplicate-editions.js. Add an entry only after manually confirming the editions are NOT duplicates.
$review_needed: The pairs below were flagged during the 2026-04 bulk duplicate audit. They are allowlisted to unblock CI but each one should be revisited: decide whether to consolidate under one event_id, or confirm they really are distinct races that happen to share finishers/timing data.

pairs.note: Data bug: nagaragawa_aki (autumn) references nagaragawa_102's (summer) TSV. Needs a separate result.tsv for the 秋 edition.
Same Hokkaido race registered under two event_ids (brand name vs city).
Likely the same race (Minami = South in Japanese). Candidate for consolidation.
IRONMAN Japan is held at Minami Hokkaido — cross-branding duplicate. Candidate for consolidation.
IRONMAN Japan / South Hokkaido — cross-branding duplicate. Candidate for consolidation together with minami_hokkaido.
Same race registered under prefecture-scale (Sendai) and municipal (Shichigahama) names.
Unexpected 100% overlap in 2024 — investigate whether one event_id has the wrong TSV.
Omihachiman is on Biwako (Lake Biwa); likely same race under two event_ids.
Same race — 水郷 (suigo) is a prefix qualifier for Itako.
Combined event_id and gender-split event_id overlap — decide on one scheme.
Combined event_id and gender-split event_id overlap — decide on one scheme.
IRONMAN 70.3 World Championship — combined vs men-only event_id. May need to mirror with im703_wc_women.
Cross-series (full IRONMAN vs 70.3) with 80.5% overlap in 2017. Likely a miscategorized import.
